articles.author: Lorian Belanger
articles.description: Pakistani officials said Tuesday that Islamabad has proposed a second round of talks to the U.S. and Iran, while U.S. Vice President JD Vance said on the Fox News Channel that negotiations with Iran “did make some progress.”
articles.title: Live updates: Pakistan proposes new US-Iran talks as Vance and Trump hint at progress

articles.author: Kareem Chehayeb, Matthew Lee
articles.description: Lebanon and Israel have held direct diplomatic talks for the first time in decades. Tuesday's more than two-hour meeting in Washington came after more than a month of conflict between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah. The State Department praised the two sides for what it called “productive discussions.” Israel's ambassador to the U.S. hailed what he called a convergence of opinion about removing Hezbollah's influence from Lebanon. Lebanon's ambassador to the U.S. called for a ceasefire and to address the humanitarian crisis resulting from the conflict. Hezbollah opposed the direct talks and wasn’t represented. U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio called the talks a “historic opportunity.”
articles.title: Lebanon and Israel hold direct diplomatic talks brokered by US

articles.author: Josef Federman
articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has escalated tensions with Iran by announcing a blockade of all Iranian ports. He made the announcement on Monday after 21-hour-long U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks in Pakistan ended without agreement. The blockade aims to pressure Iran to open the critical and strategic Strait of Hormuz and accept a deal to end the war. Iran has responded with threats against other ports in the region, raising concerns that the ceasefire could collapse. Trump claims Iran still wants a deal, but both sides remain entrenched. Pakistan's foreign minister has expressed hope for renewed dialogue.
articles.title: Trump escalates Iran tensions with US military blockade

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Brian P. D. Hannon, Michael Warren, Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, Drew Callister, Aisha I. Jefferson, Sam Burdette, Nell Clark
articles.description: Lebanon and Israel are holding their first direct diplomatic talks in decades in Washington. U.S. President Donald Trump said Tuesday that U.S.-Iran talks may resume over the next two days.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump hints at new Iran talks as Hormuz standoff intensifies

articles.author: Mary Clare Jalonick
articles.description: Republicans in Congress are forging ahead with a risky go-it-alone strategy for funding the Department of Homeland Security. The department has now been shut down for almost two months as Democrats demand changes to President Donald Trump’s broad campaign of immigration enforcement. Senate Majority Leader John Thune said Tuesday that Republicans will try and pass the money for Immigration and Customs Enforcement “the hard way,” bypassing Democrats. President Donald Trump appeared to be on board with the strategy. But the budget process Republicans plan to use could get messy. And support from Republicans in the House is far from certain.
articles.title: Republicans adopt new strategy for funding Homeland Security

articles.author: Michael Kunzelman
articles.description: An appeals court panel has ruled that a federal judge must end his contempt investigation of the Trump administration for failing to comply with an order over flights carrying Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador last year. The divided appeals court panel made that ruling Tuesday. The panel majority said Chief Judge James Boasberg abused his discretion in forging ahead with criminal contempt proceedings stemming from the March 2025 deportation flights. Circuit Judge Neomi Rao wrote in the court’s majority opinion that President Donald Trump’s administration has a “clear and indisputable” right to the termination of the contempt proceedings.
articles.title: Appeals court orders judge to end contempt investigation of Trump administration deportation flights

articles.author: Megan Janetsky, Will Weissert
articles.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has recently been taking a firmer stance with the U.S., defying pressures where other countries have caved. This shift follows changes in geopolitical dynamics in the Americas and the Middle East, giving her more leverage. Sheinbaum has become more vocal, especially regarding the deaths of Mexican migrants in U.S. custody and regarding Cuba, a longtime ally. While she paused oil shipments to Cuba, she has also criticized the U.S. energy blockade as unjust. Her government has taken issue with conditions at U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention centers. Experts suggest her bolder tone comes from strengthened trade ties and high-level talks with Washington, focusing on security and migration, two top U.S. priorities.
articles.title: Mexico's Claudia Sheinbaum takes a firmer stance with Trump on migrant deaths and Cuba

articles.author: Sophie Austin
articles.description: A man who was shot by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers in central California last week is expected in court to face charges that he assaulted a federal officer with his vehicle. The U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Eastern District of California alleges Carlos Ivan Mendoza Hernandez struck a federal agent with his car and reversed back into a law enforcement vehicle after he was pulled over on April 7. He’s scheduled to appear in court in Sacramento on Tuesday. Mendoza’s lawyer Patrick Kolasinski has said his client panicked and tried to flee when ICE agents blocked his car. Federal officials say Mendoza is in the country illegally. Kolasinski says he doesn't know his client's status in the U.S.
articles.title: Federal prosecutors charging California man shot by ICE with assault on an officer

articles.author: Rob Maaddi
articles.description: NFL reporter Dianna Russini has resigned from The Athletic less than a week after published photos of her and New England Patriots coach Mike Vrabel at an Arizona resort prompted an internal investigation at The New York Times-owned sports outlet. The New York Post last week published the photos of Vrabel and Russini at the Sedona hotel and said they were taken before the NFL owners meetings that began in Phoenix on March 29. Russini joined The Athletic in 2023 after nearly a decade at ESPN, where she held various roles, including SportsCenter anchor, NFL analyst and insider. She hosted a podcast for The Athletic and made appearances on their video platform.
articles.title: NFL reporter Dianna Russini resigns from The Athletic

articles.author: Hannah Schoenbaum
articles.description: Democratic Rep. Eric Swalwell has announced that he’s leaving Congress and the crowded contest for California governor following sexual assault allegations that he denies. The the 45-year-old congressman had been among a group of leading Democrats in the race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom. Swalwell’s campaign unraveled within about 48 hours after the San Francisco Chronicle reported allegations that he had sexually assaulted a woman twice, including when she worked for him. Later Friday, CNN reported that other women accused him of various forms of sexual misconduct. Swalwell’s decision to leave Congress quickly followed and came as bipartisan lawmakers pushed for a vote to expel him.
articles.title: What to know about Swalwell leaving the House and the governor's race
